title: Europol's Operation Saffron takes down First VPN service over ransomware attacks 33 'bulletproof' servers spread across 27 countries seized
summary: Europol's Operation Saffron took down First VPN service for facilitating ransomware attacks. Thirty-three 'bulletproof' servers were seized across 27 countries. The operation targeted the company's involvement in cybercrime.
insight: Ransomware attacks compromised VPN's promise of online anonymity and security.

title: Open-source non-profit claims Bambu Lab violated license SFC steps in after multi-billion dollar 3D printer giant threatened independent developer, issued cease-and-desist demand on OrcaSlicer fork that restored cloud printing features
summary: Bambu Lab is accused of violating the AGPLv3 license by including proprietary code alongside open-source software. This alleged violation was discovered after the company threatened an independent developer, issuing a cease-and-desist demand for a fork of OrcaSlicer that restored cloud printing features. The Software Freedom Conservancy (SFC) says this practice has been ongoing for years.
insight: License compliance crucial for open-source project integrity and independence.

title: Nvidia's memory costs soar 485%, latest AI systems now cost $7.8 million to build memory now comprises 25% of the total cost, Rubin GPUs a mere $50,000 apiece
summary: Nvidia's memory costs surge 485%, impacting AI system costs significantly. Memory now accounts for 25% of the total cost in Vera Rubin platforms. This shift affects the overall pricing of the systems.
insight: Rising memory costs drastically inflate AI system development expenses.

title: EU forced to exempt banned Chinese chipmaker after auto industry warns of supply crisis European car factories warn of imminent supply chain collapse
summary: The European Commission will propose a temporary exemption for a Chinese chipmaker from EU sanctions, due to warnings from the auto industry about an impending supply chain crisis.
insight: Automakers fear supply collapse without Chinese semiconductor access.

title: Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling hunts three fugitives for document forgery, fraudulent declarations in Super Micro smuggling case
summary: Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling, targeting three fugitives for document forgery and fraudulent declarations in relation to the Super Micro smuggling case. This move makes it even more difficult to smuggle Hopper or Blackwell chips into mainland data centers. The crackdown aims to tighten regulations on high-tech exports.
insight: Taiwan's crackdown tightens Nvidia chip smuggling regulations significantly globally.

title: AMD begins production ramp of 256-core EPYC Venice first 2nm HPC chip claims 70% performance leap
summary: AMD has started mass production of its 256-core EPYC Venice processor, utilizing TSMC's 2nm N2 process. This marks a significant leap in performance, with the Venice chip claiming a 70% performance increase. The 6th Gen EPYC processor is designed for high-performance computing applications.
insight: Breakthrough in high-performance computing with unprecedented performance gains.

title: MSI MPG 322UR QD-OLED X24 4K 240 Hz gaming monitor review: Blistering performance with pro-level color
summary: The MSI MPG 322UR QD-OLED X24 gaming monitor delivers exceptional performance and color accuracy, rivaling pro displays.
insight: Cutting-edge gaming performance with top-tier color accuracy achieved.

title: Lenovo G02 retro handheld allegedly comes preloaded with thousands of copyrighted games, including Nintendo ROMs company confirms that it s an officially white-labeled device meant for the Chinese market
summary: Lenovo confirmed partnering with a third-party to sell the G02 retro handheld in China. The device allegedly comes preloaded with thousands of copyrighted games, including Nintendo ROMs. The legitimacy of these titles' licenses is in question.
insight: Licensing disputes could impact the global games industry significantly.

title: ESP32-S31 development boards unveiled for IoT, Smart Audio, and HMI applications
summary: ESP32-S31 development boards unveiled for IoT, Smart Audio, and HMI applications, offering advanced features like Gigabit Ethernet, 2.4 GHz WiFi 6, and RISC-V cores.
insight: Advances in IoT, Smart Audio, and HMI with high-performance RISC-V cores.

title: Huion Note E A 8.4-inch Android 15 electronic notebook with battery-free, pressure sensitive stylus
summary: The Huion Note E is an 8.4-inch Android 15 electronic notebook with a battery-free, pressure-sensitive stylus. It features a MediaTek Helio G99 SoC, 6GB of memory, and 128GB of storage. The device provides an e-Paper-like experience with a 60 Hz refresh rate.
insight: Combines traditional pen-on-paper experience with modern Android functionality seamlessly.

title: M5Stack StopWatch ESP32-S3 devkit offers 1.75-inch touch AMOLED, microphone, speaker, and GPIO expansion
summary: M5Stack Core StopWatch is a portable IoT development board with a 1.75-inch AMOLED display, speaker, microphone, and expansion capabilities.
insight: Enables compact, voice-enabled IoT devices with AI acceleration capabilities.

title: reComputer RK3576/RK3588 Edge AI computers are supported by reComputer AI Lab one-click deployment platform
summary: Seeed Studio's reComputer RK3576/RK3588 Edge AI computers offer one-click AI deployment through the reComputer AI Lab platform, featuring Rockchip RK3576 and RK3588 processors with advanced AI, video, and networking capabilities.
insight: Simplified AI deployment accelerates edge AI innovation development.

title: Ouster lidar for Gecko robots improves detect and repair
summary: Ouster's Rev8 lidar sensors will enhance Gecko Robotics' Komodo robots, providing more precise insights into infrastructure, aircraft, and ship cracks.
insight: Advanced sensing improves crack detection and infrastructure integrity assessment.

title: Steve Wozniak mentions AI in graduation speech and gets cheers instead of boos
summary: Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak spoke at Grand Valley State University's graduation ceremony, mentioning AI without backlash.
insight: Tech icon's positive reception showcases evolving AI public perception

title: Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti vs AMD Radeon 9070 XT: Massive 52 Game Benchmark
summary: We compare the Radeon RX 9070 XT and GeForce RTX 5070 Ti in 52 games, evaluating performance at 1440p and 4K with both rasterization and ray tracing enabled.
insight: Real-world performance differences between Nvidia and AMD in modern games revealed.

title: SpaceX files for record-breaking IPO with rockets, AI, and Mars ambitions at the center
summary: SpaceX files for a record-breaking IPO after reporting significant revenue growth. However, the company has also posted substantial losses in 2026, following a profitable year in 2024. The losses continue a trend seen in the first quarter of 2026.
insight: Revenue growth comes with significant operational losses for SpaceX.

title: Lenovo-branded retro handheld comes pre-loaded with tons of Nintendo ROMs
summary: Lenovo partnered with a Chinese company to release a retro handheld gaming device pre-loaded with Nintendo ROMs, despite not being directly involved in its manufacturing or sales.
insight: Lenovo's brand licensing strategy expands gaming options in China's market.

title: Google accidentally published a four-year-old Chromium security bug, then tried to hide it again
summary: Google briefly exposed and then hid a four-year-old Chromium security bug. The vulnerability was initially discovered in 2022 and still requires a fix. Researcher Lyra Rebane first identified the issue four years ago.
insight: Unpatched security bugs can put users at risk for extended periods.

title: Samsung's new 32-inch 6K gaming monitor is the first of its kind if you ignore the one Samsung made last year
summary: Samsung's Odyssey G80HS features a 32-inch 6K IPS panel with 165Hz refresh rate.
insight: First 32-inch 6K gaming monitor with high refresh rate available

title: Intel says 14A is on track for 2028 risk production, 10A and 7A now in development
summary: Intel's 14A process node is on track for 2028 risk production, with volume manufacturing expected in 2029, aligning with TSMC's A14 roadmap.
insight: Significant progress in Intel's chip manufacturing technology development plans

title: Intuit cuts 3,000 jobs as it refocuses on AI, but says layoffs are "not about AI"
summary: Intuit cuts 3,000 jobs to simplify structure and focus on key bets, including AI.
insight: Rapid AI adoption forces companies to reevaluate and simplify operations.

title: Vivaldi 8 brings a redesigned interface and smarter tab tools
summary: Vivaldi 8 revamps its desktop interface for faster navigation and deeper customization.
insight: Enhanced user experience boosts productivity and browser efficiency significantly.

title: China built a gaming GPU: Lisuan's LX 7G100 performs like an RTX 3060 but costs almost $500
summary: China's Lisuan has released a gaming GPU, the LX 7G100, which outperforms an RTX 3060 but costs nearly $500.
insight: Chinese GPU maker challenges NVIDIA's dominance with affordable high-performance option.
